text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 21 September 2025 

Dr Patel  
Riverside Family Practice  
22 Brook Street, London SW1A 1AB  

Dear Dr Patel,  

Emma Collins, DOB: 03-04-1993, Hospital No: U908311 NHS No. 4432117765  
Flat 5, 12 Hawthorn Court, London N7 8QP 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Epilepsy of uncertain characteristics/post-partum within the last six months/fragmented night-time sleep/iron deficiency under GP care/no alcohol/non-smoker. 

Medication: Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily.  
Folic acid 5 mg daily (ongoing).  
Ferrous fumarate 210 mg once daily. 

I reviewed Emma Collins in clinic today. She attended with her partner and their infant. She is six months post-partum and reports disrupted nocturnal sleep due to infant care but is otherwise well. Mood stable; no red flags for post-natal depression. She is breastfeeding; supply adequate and she has had counselling regarding lamotrigine monitoring in breastfeeding. No intercurrent illnesses. 

Seizures: She and her partner keep a shared digital record. They report no seizures since the late third trimester, and none at all since delivery. She describes the current phase as a continued period without events, despite sleep fragmentation. No auras, blackouts, or witnessed episodes. There have been no injuries and no emergency attendances. 

Examination: Observations normal. Neurological examination unremarkable. No focal deficits. 

Plan:  
- Continue lamotrigine at current dose; no changes today.  
- Routine safety advice reiterated regarding sleep management and adherence.  
- Bloods: check lamotrigine level and FBC/UE/LFTs via GP in 2–3 weeks, as breastfeeding continues.  
- Follow-up in 6 months, sooner if any events occur. 

Yours sincerely,

entities.text: They report no seizures since the late third trimester, and none at all since delivery.

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Clinic date is 21 September 2025. The letter states she is six months post-partum and has had no seizures since delivery. Therefore, she has been seizure-free for approximately 6 months, meeting the ≥6-month criterion for seizure-free status. No other seizure frequencies are given.
